req.title: Patient portal login
req.statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-023 mandates SSO-based patient login with session establishment on success and rate-limiting (HTTP 429) on excessive failed attempts. Four tests are designed: (1) EP positive – valid SSO assertion establishes a session; (2) NEG negative – invalid SSO assertion is rejected without a session; (3) BVA boundary – exactly N failed attempts triggers 429 at the rate-limit threshold; (4) STC state-transition – session transitions from unauthenticated → authenticated → invalidated on logout, covering the full auth state machine. Each test resets state via fixtures to remain idempotent.

tests.name: test_valid_sso_login_establishes_session
preconditions: Patient account exists with a valid SSO binding
No prior failed attempts for this patient
actions: POST /portal/login with a valid SSO assertion token
Capture the HTTP response status and Set-Cookie header
expected: HTTP 200 or 302 redirect to portal home
Response sets a session cookie (e.g. portal_session)
Session cookie has Secure and HttpOnly flags
trace: R-HLT-023: patient shall log in using SSO
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite.local"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_rate_limit(clear_rate_limit_fixture):
    clear_rate_limit_fixture("valid_patient")

def test_valid_sso_login_establishes_session(valid_sso_assertion):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/portal/login",
        json={"sso_assertion": valid_sso_assertion},
        allow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 302), (
        f"Expected 200 or 302, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    cookie = resp.cookies.get("portal_session")
    assert cookie is not None, "portal_session cookie not set"
    raw = resp.headers.get("Set-Cookie", "")
    assert "HttpOnly" in raw, "Cookie missing HttpOnly flag"
    assert "Secure" in raw, "Cookie missing Secure flag"

tests.name: test_invalid_sso_assertion_rejected
preconditions: Tampered or expired SSO assertion is prepared
No prior failed attempts for this patient (below rate-limit)
actions: POST /portal/login with a tampered/invalid SSO assertion
Capture the HTTP response status and body
expected: No session cookie is set in the response
Response body contains an error message
trace: R-HLT-023: failed attempts shall notify the patient
R-HLT-023: error pathway – invalid SSO assertion

tests.name: test_rate_limit_triggers_at_threshold
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N is known (default: 5 attempts per window)
Rate-limit counter is reset to zero for the patient
actions: POST /portal/login N-1 times with an invalid SSO assertion (expect 401 each time)
POST /portal/login a Nth time with an invalid SSO assertion
expected: Nth attempt returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Patient notification is triggered (email/SMS flag in response or side-effect log)
trace: R-HLT-023: failed attempts shall be rate-limited
R-HLT-023: error pathway – 429 when too many attempts
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite.local"
RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD = 5

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_state(clear_rate_limit_fixture):
    clear_rate_limit_fixture("rate_limit_patient")
    yield
    clear_rate_limit_fixture("rate_limit_patient")

def test_rate_limit_triggers_at_threshold(invalid_sso_assertion, rate_limit_patient_assertion):
    for attempt in range(1, RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD):
        r = requests.post(
            f"{BASE_URL}/portal/login",
            json={"sso_assertion": rate_limit_patient_assertion},
            allow_redirects=False,
        )
        assert r.status_code == 401, (
            f"Attempt {attempt}: expected 401, got {r.status_code}"
        )
    final = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/portal/login",
        json={"sso_assertion": rate_limit_patient_assertion},
        allow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert final.status_code == 429, (
        f"Attempt {RATE_LIMIT_THRESHOLD}: expected 429, got {final.status_code}"
    )
    assert "Retry-After" in final.headers, "429 must include Retry-After header"

tests.name: test_session_state_transitions_login_to_logout
preconditions: Patient account exists with valid SSO binding
No active session exists for the patient
actions: Verify unauthenticated state: GET /portal/home returns 401 or 302 to login
POST /portal/login with valid SSO assertion (transition: unauthenticated → authenticated)
Verify authenticated state: GET /portal/home with session cookie returns 200
POST /portal/logout with session cookie (transition: authenticated → invalidated)
Verify invalidated state: GET /portal/home with old session cookie returns 401 or 302
expected: Post-logout: /portal/home returns 401 or 302, session cookie rejected
trace: R-HLT-023: patient shall log in using SSO
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite.local"

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_rate_limit(clear_rate_limit_fixture):
    clear_rate_limit_fixture("valid_patient")

def test_session_state_transitions_login_to_logout(valid_sso_assertion):
    pre = requests.get(f"{BASE_URL}/portal/home", allow_redirects=False)
    assert pre.status_code in (401, 302), "Pre-login must block /portal/home"

    login = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/portal/login",
        json={"sso_assertion": valid_sso_assertion},
        allow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert login.status_code in (200, 302)
    session_cookie = login.cookies.get("portal_session")
    assert session_cookie is not None

    authed = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/portal/home",
        cookies={"portal_session": session_cookie},
        allow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert authed.status_code == 200, "Authenticated user must access /portal/home"

    logout = requests.post(
        f"{BASE_URL}/portal/logout",
        cookies={"portal_session": session_cookie},
    )
    assert logout.status_code in (200, 204)

    post_logout = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/portal/home",
        cookies={"portal_session": session_cookie},
        allow_redirects=False,
    )
    assert post_logout.status_code in (401, 302), "Invalidated session must not access /portal/home"
